title: GarageHUD Work-List — the scoreboard
purpose: The single durable plan of record. Completed work is kept with its evidence (commit + test delta) so it can't fade; open work is kept with explicit completion and testing criteria so skipped work has nowhere to hide. This file is the source of truth for what is done and what is next — not memory, not the commit log alone.

session_protocol._note: Every session reads this file FIRST, before touching code. Follow these steps in order.
steps: 1. Read this whole file. The scoreboard, not recollection, defines the current state.

2. Choose the next item: the first item (top-to-bottom) whose status is 'incomplete' AND whose every depends_on id is 'complete' AND which is not 'blocked'. Honor an explicit user request over this default, but never silently reorder.
3. Set that item's status to 'in_progress' before starting work.
4. Do the work until EVERY string in completion_criteria is objectively met.
5. Run the tests named in testing_criteria (default: the full suite). Work is not done until they pass.
6. Record evidence in result: the test delta (e.g. '339 -> 342, +3'), the commit hash, the date (YYYY-MM-DD), and any deliberate trade-off. Then set status to 'complete'.

7. Never set 'complete' without a recorded, passing test result. Partial or unverified work stays 'incomplete', or 'blocked' with a blocked_reason. No exceptions — 'main is always green' (see CONTRIBUTING.md).
8. If new work is discovered, add it as a new item with status 'incomplete' rather than doing it silently.

status_values.incomplete: Not started, or started and not yet verified complete. The default for all new work.
status_values.blocked: Cannot proceed; blocked_reason states why (e.g. needs real hardware, awaiting owner decision).
status_values.complete: All completion_criteria met and testing_criteria passed, with evidence recorded in result.

conventions.test_command: cd GarageHUDKit && swift build 2>&1 | grep -cE 'error:|warning:'  # must print 0  &&  swift test  # all pass
conventions.green_bar: Zero warnings (-strict-concurrency=complete) and zero failing tests before any commit.
conventions.device_deploy: Use -configuration Debug for Tim's phone. Debug sets ASSETCATALOG_COMPILER_APPICON_NAME=AppIconDev (the papaya car-first icon); Release sets AppIcon and silently replaces the dev icon with the production one, destroying the on-device signal that tells a dev build apart. Installing Release over the dev build reverted the icon on 2026-07-19 — do not repeat it. Command: xcodebuild -project GarageHUD-iOS/GarageHUD.xcodeproj -scheme GarageHUD -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS,id=00008130-001228121120001C' -allowProvisioningUpdates build ; then xcrun devicectl device install app --device 00008130-001228121120001C <app from Debug-iphoneos> ; then xcrun devicectl device process launch --device 00008130-001228121120001C com.vanlaw.GarageHUD. Build Release only to verify it compiles — never to install on the dev phone.
conventions.roles: Vector (AI) does all build/test/install/commit/sync. Tim unlocks the phone and pushes. Never hand Tim multi-step click instructions.

field_findings_2026_07_19.device: iPhone 15 Pro Max, iOS 26.6, developer mode enabled. Initial findings came from a Release build of 43635eb; all re-validation after the W-061 and W-062 fixes was on Debug builds (Debug is the correct configuration for this phone — it carries AppIconDev).

field_findings_2026_07_19.honors_confidence_bands: FAIL, then PASS after the W-061 fix — first attempt summed a [Strong evidence] measured 381 whp with a [estimate] ~201 whp gain and reported the total as fact. result.tests: 465 -> 480 across the session, +15; zero warnings under -strict-concurrency=complete
result.notes: First real-hardware validation of the conversational Steward, on Tim's iPhone 15 Pro Max (iOS 26.6, Debug build with the dev icon). PASSES: StewardAssistant.isLLMAvailable resolved true on device, proving all three gates (canImport FoundationModels, iOS 26 availability, SystemLanguageModel runtime availability) clear together; roughly 1.2s to a full on-device answer; out-of-record refusal is clean — asked for a Hellcat torque curve it named the 2008 Forester XT Sport and denied the premise without inventing a figure; speech-in transcribes against the real mic and now submits in one gesture. Two failures were found by asking real questions and both were fixed and re-validated in the same session: confidence-band honesty (W-061, summed a measured figure with a derived estimate) and the speech-in send gesture (W-062). Neither was reachable by the test suite — the record was accurate line by line, and it was the adjacency of two facts that lied. Cloud voice was split to W-060 and is deferred by owner decision so GarageHUD stays free to run; it is not part of this item. Still open and NOT claimed here: the LLM path never carries a typed confidence band (StewardLLM returns StewardReply(text:) with confidence nil), so the evidence chip that renders for keyword answers never appears for LLM answers — awaiting Tim's defect-or-deliberate ruling.

items.title: Steward must not do arithmetic on record figures — measured + estimated gain was summed
items.why: Field-found on device 2026-07-19 (Tim, first real LLM Steward session). Asked how much power the car makes, the Steward took the 381 whp measured dyno figure and ADDED the ~201 whp 'gained over stock' estimate, reporting a number that appears nowhere in the record. horsepowerGainedOverStock is DERIVED from the measurement (dyno - estimatedStockWheelHP, Vehicle.swift:607), so the gain is already contained in the 381 — the two are not additive. StewardGrounding.record lists them as adjacent sibling facts with no stated relationship, and StewardGrounding.instructions never forbids deriving new numbers. This is a truth-doctrine violation: the assistant invented a figure and stated it as fact.
completion_criteria: The grounding record states the containment relationship explicitly — the gain is part of the measured figure, not additional to it
instructions forbid computing new figures by combining recorded values; report figures as recorded
A regression test pins that the record text cannot be read as inviting measured + gain addition
Re-validated on device: asking 'how much power does it make?' returns the measured figure without summing
testing_criteria: full package suite green, plus the new grounding regression test; then on-device re-ask

result.notes: Root cause: horsepowerGainedOverStock is derived (measured - estimatedStockWheelHP, Vehicle.swift:607) but StewardGrounding.record emitted it as a bare sibling fact beside the measured figure, with no rule against arithmetic. Fix has three parts: (1) the measured line is labelled as the car's total at the wheels, (2) the gain names its containment explicitly ('~N whp of that 381 whp ... already included ... NOT additional ... Do not add these together'), (3) a general instruction forbids combining recorded values, since derived figures already contain each other. Extended to the Investment section unprompted: documentedReconcile is an alternative accounting and pricedSoFar is a portion of the total, so the identical additive trap existed for the owner's money and is now labelled too. Device-validated by Tim on the Debug build: 'how much power does it make?' returned 381, and the deliberate trap follow-up ('so what's the total with the gain?') did not produce a summed number. Honest limit: these are instructions to a language model plus tests pinning the record's wording — the tests constrain the prompt, not the model's obedience, so this is mitigation rather than a hard arithmetic guard.

items.title: Speech-in has no send affordance — the mic must be un-tapped to submit
items.why: Field-found on device 2026-07-19. Tapping the mic prompts for permission, then listens and transcribes correctly, but there is no way to send the utterance — the owner must tap the mic a second time to stop listening before the question becomes active. That is two gestures for one intent and breaks Tim's standing rule (DD-004, 'one gesture, one response'). Recognition itself works against the real mic; the interaction around it does not.
completion_criteria: Speaking a question and stopping naturally submits it without a second deliberate gesture on the mic
The behavior reads as one gesture, one response — no new affordance vocabulary introduced
Deterministic logic covered by tests where it is testable without hardware
testing_criteria: full package suite green; on-device confirmation by Tim that one gesture sends

result.notes: Root cause was not a missing control: finish(with:) already submitted the utterance, but it only ran on result.isFinal, and stop() calls task?.cancel() which discards the pending final result. The second mic tap therefore destroyed the very path that would have sent the question. toggle() now routes a user-initiated stop through stopAndSubmit(), which asks what has already been transcribed instead of waiting for a final result cancellation guarantees will never arrive. No new button and no new affordance vocabulary — the mic tap simply does what it already looked like it did (DD-004). Saying nothing still cancels rather than firing an empty exchange, pinned by SpeechSubmissionTests. Honest limit: the pure submit rule is unit-tested, but the mic round-trip needs real hardware, so Tim's device confirmation is the evidence.

items.title: Screen sleeps during a live session — dials stop being watchable
items.why: Field-found on device 2026-07-19 during live OBD testing. The iPhone follows its normal idle timer and sleeps while a live session is measuring, so the owner cannot watch the dials. isIdleTimerDisabled appears nowhere in the codebase, so nothing ever requests wake. A live telemetry view the phone turns off is not usable in the driveway; this directly undercuts W-014 field work.
completion_criteria: The screen stays awake while a live session is actively measuring
Wake is released when the session stops, backgrounds, or errors — never left on globally
The idle-timer decision is driven by session state, not by view lifetime alone
testing_criteria: full package suite green; on-device confirmation that dials stay visible through a measuring session and the phone sleeps normally afterward

result.notes: New pure policy ScreenWake.shouldStayAwake(sessionRunning:connection:) decides from session state rather than view lifetime, applied in LiveSessionView via UIApplication.isIdleTimerDisabled behind canImport(UIKit). Holds wake through bring-up (scanning/handshaking) and through degraded/reconnecting — those are precisely when the owner is watching — and releases on .disconnected or a stopped session. stop() releases unconditionally so a session ending by button, error, or view teardown cannot strand the screen awake. Device-validated by Tim with Auto-Lock at 30s on the simulated feed (no car required): screen stayed lit with dials moving through a running session, and slept normally after Stop. Deliberate asymmetry pinned by test: OBDConnectionState.isLive stays strictly .polling (the only state where values may be called measured) while wake covers the whole engaged session.

items.title: TD-001 phase 2a — stamped merge layer (ADR-0005 accepted and implemented)
items.why: Scalars, parts, and maintenance are still adopt-side-wins, so concurrent edits are a coin flip — the pattern live telemetry made routine (phone writes during driveway sessions while the Mac holds spec edits). ADR-0005 designs the next step: hybrid-logical-clock stamps rather than untrustworthy device wall-clock time, and coherence GROUPS rather than free-floating fields, because field-level LWW can synthesize a vehicle that never existed on either device (a crank horsepower figure wearing a wheel basis label) — the same adjacency failure that produced W-061.
completion_criteria: ADR-0005 accepted by Tim (or amended and then accepted) — the coherence grouping is a judgment call and must be his
All ten tests in the ADR's test plan exist and pass, written before the merge implementation
Merging A into B and B into A produce identical results for every case
Legacy unstamped documents merge byte-identically to today's adopt-side-wins behaviour
W-054 append preservation and W-056 tombstone suites stay green without modification
testing_criteria: full package suite green with the ten new merge tests; no changes required to existing sync suites

result.notes: ADR-0005 accepted by Tim as written. Implemented: SyncStamp (hybrid logical clock — physical time is only a floor, peers adopt any higher reading, node id is the deterministic final tiebreak), SyncClock with an injected wall clock, CoherenceGroup (identity/power/money/status/capability), Vehicle.groupStamps, per-record stamps on Part and MaintenanceItem, and stamp-aware resolution in GarageMerge. All ten ADR tests pass, including commutativity (merging A into B and B into A agree) and the legacy case (two unstamped sides still resolve adopt-side-wins, byte-identical to before). The W-054 append and W-056 tombstone suites passed untouched; a tombstone still outranks a newer stamped edit. Schema bumped 1 -> 2: a v1 client can decode a v2 document but would strip stamps on its next write and silently resurrect the races, so refusal via .unsupportedVersion is the honest failure — every device must run a v2 build. Bug caught by the plan's own test 8: Vehicle, Part AND MaintenanceItem each hand-write init(from:), while encoding is synthesized, so stamps encoded fine and vanished on read until each decoder was updated by hand; the round-trip test now covers all three, because testing two of the three is how the third stays broken. HONEST SCOPE LIMIT: no production code calls setStamp yet, so every stamp is .zero in practice and runtime behaviour is unchanged from before this commit. The merge layer is correct and inert. Activation is W-065.

items.title: TD-001 phase 2b — stamp edits on save so the merge layer actually engages
items.why: W-064 built and tested the stamped merge, but nothing writes stamps: every stamp is .zero, so concurrent edits still resolve adopt-side-wins exactly as before. The machinery is inert until edits are stamped. Proposed approach is to stamp centrally in GarageStore on save by diffing the saved vehicle against the previously persisted one, rather than touching every editing view — one place to be correct, and no chance a new editor forgets. Needs a design decision from Tim before implementation: central diff-on-save vs explicit stamping at each edit site.
completion_criteria: Editing a field in a coherence group stamps that group, and only that group
A save that changes nothing does not advance any stamp
Editing a part or maintenance item stamps that record only
The device node id is stable across launches and distinct per device
Two devices editing different groups concurrently both keep their edits end to end
Full suite green including the existing W-064 merge tests unchanged
testing_criteria: full package suite green; then a real two-device check with Tim — edit a spec on the Mac and a different group on the phone, sync, and confirm both survive

field_findings.implemented: SyncStamper diffs a save against the state it replaced and stamps only what changed. Wired into GarageStore.vehicles.didSet using oldValue, guarded so it never runs while loading or applying a remote document — restamping a received document would make this device the author of edits it merely received and win every race by syncing last. Re-entrancy is handled: the stamping write re-enters didSet, an isStamping flag makes the second pass a no-op, and the assignment is skipped entirely when nothing changed.
field_findings.identity: SyncIdentity persists a stable per-install node id and the clock's last reading in UserDefaults. Both matter: a node id regenerated per launch makes the tiebreak meaningless, and a clock reset on launch restarts the counter at zero so an edit made just after opening could order BEHIND one made before closing — losing the owner's newest work.
field_findings.group_detection: Vehicle.groupMatches is defined in terms of adopt() rather than a second hand-written field list, so adding a field to a coherence group updates detection and merging together. A duplicated list is exactly how a field ends up merged but never detected as edited.
field_findings.tests: 500 -> 509, +9. Covers: an edit stamps its group and only its group; a no-op save advances nothing; repeated saves are stable (a stamped record must not look changed merely because it was stamped, or the clock runs away); one part edit stamps only that part; a new vehicle needs no stamps; two groups stamped in one save get distinct increasing stamps; and the end-to-end property — two devices editing different groups both keep their edits, in both merge directions.
field_findings.not_yet_validated: No two-device run. The unit-level property holds, but nothing has been observed surviving a real CloudKit round trip between the Mac and the phone. Also unexercised: the schema 1->2 bump in practice, which means a v1 Mac will REFUSE a v2 document rather than sync with it, so both devices must be rebuilt before any real test.

items.title: iPhone text does not respond to Dynamic Type — 10pt labels are fixed
items.why: Field-found 2026-07-19: several sections are too small to read comfortably on the phone. Root cause is not sizing taste but an opt-out: HUDTheme.monoFont uses fixed .system(size:) values (title 30, body 14, label 10), and fixed point sizes ignore Dynamic Type completely. Raising text size in iOS Settings changes nothing in GarageHUD. Pinch-to-zoom would treat the symptom; the OS mechanism is the fix, and it also serves the owner reading a spec in a dim garage with oily hands.
completion_criteria: Typography scales with the owner's iOS text-size setting
The monospaced HUD character of the design language is preserved — this is not a restyle
Layouts survive the largest accessibility sizes without truncation or overlap on iPhone
Tim approves the approach before it lands, since it touches every screen
testing_criteria: full package suite green; on-device check at default, large, and an accessibility text size
items.recommendation: Map the three roles onto text styles with monospaced design (e.g. .system(.body, design: .monospaced)) or Font.custom(size:relativeTo:), so sizes scale from the owner's setting while the mono look is kept. Deliberately NOT done unilaterally: it changes exact sizes on every screen and the design language is Tim's call.

items.title: Car-page hero crops the photo — fixed-height band with fill and clip
items.why: Field-found 2026-07-19: photos are cut off on the car page. VehicleIdentitySurface.photoBand pins a height (176 compact / 230 regular), scales the image with .scaledToFill() and .clipped(), so any photo whose aspect ratio differs from the band loses its top and bottom. A landscape shot of a car loses roof and wheels — the subject, in an app whose whole point is looking at the car.
completion_criteria: A car photo is legible on the car page without losing the vehicle itself
The identity block stays visually calm — no letterboxed grey bars fighting the palette
Behaviour is predictable across portrait and landscape source photos
Tim chooses the approach: fit-with-treatment, a taller band, or an owner-set focal point
testing_criteria: on-device check with a real landscape and a real portrait photo from Tim's library
items.recommendation: Three honest options: (1) scaledToFit with a blurred or panel-toned backdrop — nothing cropped, keeps the band height; (2) keep fill but let the owner set the focal point per photo, which is the most work and the most control; (3) raise the band height and accept a gentler crop. Option 1 is the smallest change that stops losing the subject.

result.notes: First-reviewer eye-gravity pass: Garage reviewed from a live Simulator screenshot, other screens from code. Findings recorded in docs/DESIGN_DEBT.md (DD-001). Landed F1 (clear fix): the fleet-health strip clipped SERVICE DUE behind a non-obvious horizontal scroll; now wraps to two rows via ViewThatFits, verified on device before/after. items.title: Model vehicle purchase price (acquisition cost), distinct from build investment
items.why: GarageHUD has no purchase-price field — the only money fields are build/mod spend (documentedTotalInvestment, part costs). For a mostly-stock utility truck like the Tundra, what you paid is the primary money fact and it has nowhere to live, so Tim reasonably put '60K purchase price' in a free-text Note, which never becomes a number. Notes are prose; the figure can't surface.
completion_criteria: Vehicle gains a purchasePrice: Double? (tolerant decode)
Editable on Specs and shown as its own figure (not silently folded into 'Total Invested', which stays build/mod spend)
DECISION PENDING (Tim): whether to also show a combined 'all-in' (purchase + build) line, and whether purchase price appears on the fleet sheet
Honest wording: 'Purchase price' / 'paid', separate from 'invested'
testing_criteria: VehicleModelTests: purchasePrice round-trips and does not alter totalInvested
full suite green

result.notes: Vehicle.purchasePrice added (tolerant decode, older files -> nil). Shown as its own 'Purchase Price' line under a new OWNERSHIP section on Specs, editable, kept out of totalInvested. Separate lines, no all-in. Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred.

items.decision: Tim chose (2026-07-16): separate lines (no all-in combined line). Fleet-sheet inclusion deferred unless asked.

items.title: Honesty audit: a numberless dyno was labeled measured
items.why: Adversarial audit — a .dyno record with no wheelHorsepower made currentHorsepowerEstimate fall back to the CRANK factory figure while 'measured' was gated on record TYPE, so the build sheet and fleet sheet (both shareable) stated e.g. '155 whp measured' for a car that never measured wheel power. Crank shown as wheel, estimate shown as measured. Adjacent to W-004, which fixed only the Build-Plan number and left the label paths leaky.
completion_criteria: measuredWheelHorsepower + hasMeasuredPower on Vehicle (a real dyno number is the only thing that counts as measured)
currentHorsepowerEstimate = measuredWheelHorsepower ?? factory (no crank-via-numberless-dyno, no shadowing an earlier real dyno)
every measured label gated on hasMeasuredPower, not record type (build sheet, fleet sheet, BuildProgress, dashboard torque)
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests: numberless dyno never labeled measured; earlier real dyno not shadowed; installed part overrides confirmed-stock; planned part not counted as covered
full suite green

result.notes: Empirically confirmed the lie ('155 whp measured'), fixed, and locked with regression tests. Audit also cleared attacks 2 (installed part overrides stale stock claim) and 3 (planned part reads 'undocumented, planned', not 'covered') as honest. Attack 4 (absurd user-entered dyno reported raw) is garbage-in, not an engine lie — possible future sanity-flag, not tracked. UPDATE 2026-07-17: attack 4 was overruled by the Fable review (W-022) — a non-positive dyno figure no longer counts as a measurement.

items.title: Fable review fixes — six proven honesty lies, three integrity bugs
items.why: Independent adversarial review (Fable, 2026-07-17) empirically proved the app stating falsehoods: crank labeled 'whp' in BuildAssessment.powerSummary (third instance of the W-004/W-021 class); wishlist costs reported as spend on Specs and in LLM grounding; a 3,000-mi-overdue truck producing zero Steward observations ('Nothing stands out right now.') while the briefing header called it overdue; gap evidence denying a logged wishlist part; grounding denying a logged numberless dyno; '-50 whp (measured)' on the shareable build sheet. Plus: a fresh device could clobber the whole cloud garage (conflict guard skipped when appliedCloudUpdatedAt was nil); mark-done with unknown odometer kept a stale mileage baseline (fresh service read overdue); OBD boost hardcoded sea-level baro; moveParts carried the source car's installDate into the destination's history.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.measuredDynoRecords/latestMeasuredDyno is the single source for every measured-power claim; non-positive figures never count
spendByCategory counts installed parts only
Steward.observe judges maintenance mileage-aware, same as every other surface; evidence and build-sheet copy name the actual overdue driver
supportGap acknowledges planned parts; grounding acknowledges numberless dyno sessions and labels power-to-weight basis
pushNow treats never-applied-cloud-state as a conflict (snapshot + adopt) instead of blind-pushing; stripped-remote exception preserved
markMaintenanceDone clears the mileage baseline when the odometer is unknown
OBD decoder reports raw MAP kPa, polls baro PID 0133, computes gauge boost against measured baro
moveParts re-dates the moved part to the transfer moment
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests regression per lie; PartTransferTests re-date; MileageMaintenanceTests baseline-clear; OBDPIDDecoderTests baro/gauge
full suite green

result.notes: Remaining from the review, deliberately NOT done here (needs owner/architect judgment): a table-driven cross-surface honesty sweep test (adversarial vehicle matrix x every textual surface x global assertions) — the structural guard against instance #4 of the crank-as-wheel class; a PowerFigure/money-bucket type so value+label can't be mis-paired at call sites; odometer input validation; units/locale; a UI path to discover and restore conflict snapshots. UPDATE: Tim authorized continuing — tracked as W-023..W-026.

items.title: Cross-surface honesty sweep test
items.why: Three reviews found the same value/label mis-pair (crank as 'whp', estimate as 'measured') in three different places — point fixes don't stop instance #4; a quantified invariant does. Designing the sweep immediately caught a fourth live instance: the voice Steward's power answer said 'No dyno is logged yet' for a car with a numberless dyno session on record (the H5 denial, in a surface the grounding fix missed).
completion_criteria: HonestySweepTests renders every pure textual surface (build sheet, grounding, observations, assessment, next step, four voice replies, briefing, tune readiness, build plan) for an adversarial vehicle matrix

Global assertions: whp earned or estimate-framed; 'measured' earned or negated; the record never denied; planned money always framed planned; a bare car yields no numbers; surfaces agree on overdue-ness
Mutation-verified: reintroducing the W-022 crank-as-whp bug makes the sweep fail with a pointed message
StewardConversation numberless-dyno denial fixed (same wording as grounding)
testing_criteria: HonestySweepTests green; full suite green

result.tests: 376 -> 377, +1 (one sweep test, ~110 vehicle x surface x rule checks inside)
result.notes: New textual surfaces must be added to HonestySweepTests.surfaces to inherit coverage — noted in the test header. Fleet-sheet PDF is SwiftUI-rendered and not sweepable as text; its logic reads the same shared helpers.

items.title: PowerFigure — value and honest label paired in one type
items.why: The recurring crank-as-whp bug was structural: currentHorsepowerEstimate (which can be a crank rating) and hasMeasuredPower lived apart, and every surface re-paired them by hand. Wiring the type up found FOUR MORE live view-layer instances the text sweep can't reach: VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView (the garage hero rail printed a crank rating as 'WHP'), GarageOverviewView, and FleetComparisonView (crank and wheel figures mixed in one column under a shared 'whp' header).
completion_criteria: PowerFigure { value, isMeasured, unit, qualifier, compactLabel, labeled }; Vehicle.currentPowerFigure is the display source
BuildAssessment, BuildSheetExporter, FleetSheet, SpecSheet, VehicleIdentitySurface, GarageSpotlightView, GarageOverviewView render through it
FleetComparisonView POWER column wheel-normalized (currentWheelHorsepowerEstimate) so the shared 'whp' header is true for every row
SpecSheet 'Current' labels measured vs factory and uses the figure's unit
testing_criteria: HonestyInvariantsTests PowerFigure pairing test; HonestySweepTests still green over refactored surfaces
full suite green

result.notes: View-layer visual checks (spotlight rail, identity surface, comparison grid) still deserve Tim's on-device eyeball — copy lengths changed slightly ('240 HP' where 'XXX WHP' used to render).

items.title: Odometer sanity — entry-time anomaly warnings and a self-contradiction observation
items.why: The odometer is the reasoning spine (currentMileage, milesPerDay, every mileage due state and projection derive from event readings), yet any typo was accepted silently — one slipped digit (50,000 -> 580,000) poisoned all of it with no warning and no surfacing. The review ranked this the biggest unmodeled input risk.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.odometerAnomaly(proposing:on:) flags regressions (below an already-recorded reading of the same era) and implausible rates (> 1,000 mi/day implied), era-aware for backdated entries
AddEditBuildEventView shows a calm, NON-BLOCKING caution next to the mileage field (warn, never restrict the record); editing excludes the event under edit
Steward rule data.odometerRegression surfaces a record that already disagrees with itself (later date, lower reading) as a confirmed caution
testing_criteria: OdometerSanityTests: quiet pass, first-reading pass, regression, slipped digit, road-trip day not flagged, backdated-era consistency, Steward surfacing, consistent record quiet
full suite green

result.notes: MaintenanceEditorView's baseline field intentionally not gated — it sets a schedule baseline, not a biography reading. The caution never blocks: a genuine correction (replaced cluster, fixing an old typo) must always be loggable.

items.title: Recovery — discover, export, restore preserved garage files
items.why: GarageHUD's data-safety story wrote conflict snapshots and unreadable-file backups to disk, but nothing ever listed them — the notices only showed in the session that created them, so a preserved file was effectively lost the moment the app restarted. A safety file the owner can't find is not safety.
completion_criteria: GarageStore.recoverySnapshots lists conflict snapshots, pre-restore copies, and unreadable-file backups with dates and decoded vehicle counts
GarageStore.restore(from:) replaces the garage undoably — the current garage is preserved first as a pre-restore snapshot; an unreadable file is refused and left untouched
RecoveryView (sheet from a Garage-overview recovery row shown whenever preserved files exist): export, confirm-to-restore, swipe-to-delete
restore failure surfaces an alert, never silently no-ops
testing_criteria: RecoverySnapshotTests: listing with counts, restore + pre-restore round-trip (restore the restore), unreadable refused/untouched, delete
full suite green; iOS simulator app build green

result.notes: Restore is user-confirmed in UI and always undoable at the store level. Recovery row placement (garage overview, under data-safety notices) deserves Tim's eye for DD-001 gravity; it only appears when preserved files exist.

items.title: Every NEXT line acts on tap — full-row hit target, rebuild step scrolls to its checklist

items.title: Actionability sweep — 'an instruction must carry a door', mechanized
items.why: Tim asked why the initial review missed the Fozzy dead-end. Honest answer: the review audited claims, not experiences — it verified every string and walked zero flows, and attention followed the harness (string-truth was testable, flow-completeness wasn't). Same cure as the honesty class: make the invariant enforced instead of remembered.
completion_criteria: ActionabilitySweepTests: every non-informational observation resolves to at least one concrete action; every next step carries resolvable verbs or is the documented teardown exemption

items.title: No circular verbs — resolution options reflect the knowledge state
items.why: Tim: 'confirm drivetrain is factory-stock from fozzy page not resolving… additionally, the fozzy's drivetrain was marked as stock.' Exactly: the drivetrain was ALREADY confirmed stock, and the surface offered 'confirm it's stock' anyway — a circular verb whose tap is a no-op insert, so the surface never resolves. The verbs were blind to what the record already knew.
completion_criteria: StewardResolution gap options are knowledge-aware: a confirmedAbsent (stock-confirmed) system under load offers only 'Add the <system> upgrade' — never 'confirm stock' again (applies to Steward rows and the NEXT STEP block alike)
The synthesized gap observation states the truth for its state: confirmed-stock reads 'the factory <system> is confirmed in place… carrying the added load — worth weighing', not 'isn't documented'
ActionabilitySweep gains the general rule: no offered verb may set a state that is already set (circular-verb check), with a confirmed-stock-under-load car in the matrix
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Third law joins the sweeps: never say the unearned (honesty), never ask the unanswerable (actionability), never offer the already-done (circularity).

items.title: Driveline attention is owner-calibrated: 450 whp, not +40-whp-gain
items.why: Tim, on the Fozzy's clutch nag: 'you telling me a simple stage1 upgrade on a Subaru warrants a clutch and drivetrain, no way… I won't touch the transmission till over 450HP, wouldn't touch the clutch till then.' The trigger was an invented flat gain>=40whp — flagging a stage-1 factory-turbo car (~25% bump the factory driveline was engineered for) the same as a built car at double stock. A fourth blind-spot class: honest labels on domain-uncalibrated judgment rules. The review audited whether numbers were true, never whether the thresholds were.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.drivelineAttentionWheelHP = 450 (Tim's words quoted at the definition) with powerDemandsDrivelineAttention keyed on the wheel-honest current figure
Every powerUp gate reads it: BuildAssessment relevance (drivetrain, brakes), Steward brakes-gap rule (evidence now says 'Power is past 450 whp'), fleet shared-gap brakes, BuildPlanner brake priority
Suspension-upgrade brake trigger unchanged; engine-internals rule unchanged (FI + 80 whp gain)
Test fixtures updated to sit on the correct side of the line (480 whp where scrutiny is the point)
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Effect on the real fleet: the stage-1 Fozzy stops being told it needs a clutch; the 477-whp S2K remains correctly in scope. Open question deliberately NOT built without Tim: factory-forced-induction awareness (a factory-turbo car's fueling/cooling was engineered for boost — the FI-triggered support reasoning still assumes aftermarket boost). On the board when Tim wants it.

items.title: Factory-turbocharged awareness — a factory charger is the car, not a mod
items.why: Tim: 'teach the app this platform is boosted from the factory… it's a must to understand.' The reasoning treated forced induction as always-aftermarket: a factory-turbo car with no FI part logged got NO live boost envelope, a HOLD for daring to have a boost map, and — once anything triggered assessment — 'Forced induction is installed.' wording that isn't true of a stock charger.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.hasFactoryForcedInduction: owner override (Steward Inputs toggle) ?? inference from engine description ('turbo'/'supercharged') and known factory-boosted model markers (XT, WRX, STI, Evo, GTR, Mazdaspeed, Supra, SRT4)

Two gates, one definition each: runsBoost (any boost — live envelope defaults, boost maps legitimate in the tuner) vs runsElevatedBoost (support scrutiny — aftermarket FI, or factory platform with a calibration on record or a measured gain >= 25% over stock baseline)
A SHOWROOM-STOCK factory-turbo car gets: a live boost envelope, a legitimate boost map, and ZERO support-gap nags — the factory engineered that support
Gap trigger wording is honest per path: 'Forced induction is installed.' only for aftermarket; 'running a tune (Cobb Accessport)' / 'making well over its stock power' for factory platforms

Vehicle.calibrationTerms/calibrationPartOnRecord shared by the tuner's calibration check and the elevated-boost gate (one definition of 'a tune is on record'); grounding states 'factory turbocharged… not a modification' to the LLM
testing_criteria: FactoryBoostTests: inference/override, stock-stays-quiet, tuned-earns-scrutiny with honest wording, gain-only path, envelope default, boost-map-not-a-hold, Cobb recognized
factoryTurboTuned car added to the honesty sweep matrix; full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.notes: Effect on the real Fozzy (Cobb, bolt-ons, 381 whp E85): live boost envelope by default, boost map legitimate, fueling/cooling now honestly in scope because the tune turned the boost up — phrased as 'running a tune (Cobb Accessport)', never as installed FI. Tim is checking whether the fuel pump is already logged; if it is, the fueling check reads supported.

items.title: A statistic is not a task — cost-per-hp retired from the attention stream
items.why: Tim: 'One button always in my face is "this build cost $$$$ per horsepower" Resolve? … which doesn't make it go away.' The efficiency.costPerHp observation was permanent, unresolvable arithmetic sitting in a panel captioned 'Actions only when earned', mapped to a Resolve option (Log a dyno result) that couldn't change it. The dual of the actionability law: no door on something that isn't an ask.
completion_criteria: efficiency.costPerHp removed from Steward.observe (with a NOTE at the site so it doesn't come back) and from StewardResolution / StewardRuleID

The figure keeps its real homes, caveats intact: Specs (Cost/WHP with provenance tap), the grounding record ([Moderate — wheel-estimate, not dyno-corrected]), and the voice's efficiency answer when asked
DD-004 gains the law: 'A statistic is not a task'
Pinning tests repurposed to assert the figure at its home surfaces and its permanent absence from the attention stream
testing_criteria: full suite green; deployed to Tim's device

result.tests: 419 -> 418 (net -1: one statistic-rule test retired, replacements folded into repurposed tests)
result.notes: fleet.valueLeader is the same class at fleet level (informational stat, unresolvable) — it's rarer (needs 2+ dynoed cars) and un-actionable already, so left in place but WATCHED: if Tim reports it as noise, retire it the same way.

items.title: Biography goes paper-first — white ground, ink text, restrained accent
items.why: Tim: 'as nice as the biography is, don't you think Black is a touch background to print into?' Right: the document's natural habitat is print (buyer, insurance, service folder), and a cockpit-dark page is a full-bleed toner bath. The cockpit dark is the app's identity ON SCREEN; paper conventions win on paper.
completion_criteria: BiographySheetDocument renders on a PrintTheme: white paper, ink-dark text, HUD cyan darkened to a print-legible accent, light panels with hairline strokes
Fleet sheet deliberately left dark (Tim approved its screen look, W-009); lightening it is one commit away if asked
Content untouched — same BiographyModel words, honesty sweeps unaffected
testing_criteria: full suite green (PDF render tests pass); visual print check is Tim's on a real export

items.title: Steward threshold provenance audit (Fable concern #3)
items.why: An app whose whole pitch is 'never state a guess as a fact' had a reasoning layer full of invented constants (coolant/boost limits, sequence/quiet windows, drivetrain losses, pull-fit gates) presented with the same authority as facts. Only discovery process for a wrong one was 'Tim trips over it.'
completion_criteria: Every judgment threshold in the reasoning layer enumerated with file:line, what it gates, and a provenance tag (OWNER / CONVENTION / GUESS)
A ranked calibrate-first list by consequence x arbitrariness
testing_criteria: register is accurate to the code (each entry verified against its file:line); no code change so full suite unaffected — 418 green

result.notes: docs/STEWARD_THRESHOLDS.md. Finding: exactly ONE threshold is owner-calibrated (drivelineAttentionWheelHP=450, W-044); the rest are shop convention (drivetrain losses, 30d/500mi windows, coolant 215/235) or — for eleven of them — ungrounded guesses. Top three to calibrate: per-car boost caution (currently a flat 18 psi for every boosted car, and it's a safety caution), the 14/180-day sequence/quiet windows, and the 80-whp engine-internals gate. Recommended structural follow-up: centralize into StewardThresholds with per-constant provenance so a guess can't be added without declaring itself.

items.title: Sourced per-platform live limits — boost caution + air-cooled coolant
items.why: Top calibrate-first item from the W-048 threshold audit. A flat 18 psi boost caution was applied to every boosted car regardless of setup (too high for a supercharged S2000, meaningless for the NA Tundra), and a 215/235F coolant limit was asserted on the air-cooled Baja that has no coolant at all — false authority, exactly what the app forbids.
completion_criteria: PlatformBaseline catalog: per-platform boost caution + air-cooled flag, each entry citing a source (researched from Subaru/Honda/Toyota/VW tuner communities)
OperatingEnvelope.default derives boost caution per-platform (nil if NA) and suppresses coolant limits on air-cooled engines
coolant limits made optional; live rule and Specs editor handle nil
testing_criteria: PlatformBaselineTests: S2000=13, EJ turbo=17, Tundra/Baja boost nil, air-cooled Baja has no coolant limit and emits no coolant observation even at 260F, unknown boosted -> generic 16, every catalog entry cites a source
full suite green

result.notes: Followed my rec (per-car boost caution) grounded in Tim's data (real tuner-site baselines). Sources: subaruforester.org/NASIOC (EJ255 TD04 stock ~12, safe ~16-18); S2KI/Kraftwerks (Rotrex SC ~8-12); 3UR-FE NA 381hp; VW Type-1 air-cooled. Catalog is extensible with a hard rule: a new platform must cite a source or it doesn't belong. Register updated; boost-caution guess retired.

items.title: Owner-calibrate the sequence/quiet windows (Fable concern #3, #2 calibrate-first)
items.why: Three GUESS-tagged reasoning windows from the W-048 audit — invented numbers wearing the authority of facts. Not researchable like the boost baselines; they are Tim usage calls, like the 450 driveline number.
completion_criteria: sequence-lag window owner-set (14 -> 30 days)
quiet-record window owner-set (180 -> 90 days)
flagged-pull timeout replaced with until-resolved (a later clean pull resolves it), no timer
values live in StewardThresholds with Tim/date/rationale provenance
testing_criteria: StewardWindowTests: 20d gap no flag / 40d flags; 80d quiet no / 100d yes; old breach stays, later clean pull resolves, later breach does not
existing PullReportStewardTests updated from the old-timeout assertion
full suite green

result.notes: Tim calibrated: sequence-lag 30d, quiet 90d, flagged pull until-resolved. Started the StewardThresholds home Fable recommended. Noted caveats stand as follow-ups: sequence still keys on a date gap (a 'driven between' signal would be truer) and quiet is one number for a mixed fleet (could be per-car). Explicit pull-dismiss UI is the companion to until-resolved (auto-resolves on a clean pull today).

items.title: Pre-OBD-II awareness — no live-telemetry affordance for the Baja
items.why: Tim flagged that one car (the ~1971 air-cooled VW Baja) predates OBD-II. Offering a 'connect an OBD-II adapter' affordance on a car with no OBD port is false affordance — same class as a coolant limit on an air-cooled engine.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.supportsOBD2 (US: liquid-cooled AND year>=1996; air-cooled classics false)
Live tab shows an honest unavailable notice for a pre-OBD-II car and disables scan; Simulated still works
W-014 driveway checklist excludes the Baja
testing_criteria: PlatformBaselineTests: Baja & a 1991 Civic supportsOBD2 false; S2K/Fozzy/Tundra true
full suite green

result.notes: Same honesty thread as W-049 (air-cooled coolant): the app now knows the Baja has no OBD port and says so rather than offering a connection that can never complete. Heuristic is US-year + cooling; an explicit override can follow if a car ever needs correcting.

items.title: Driveway unblock — protocol bind, per-line decode, STOPPED retry (Fable fix #1)
items.why: First real hardware session (Fozzy + Veepeak): handshake passed, ZERO PIDs decoded, degrade/reconnect loop. Two proven defects: (1) no 0100 protocol-bind step — polling began with a 1s timeout racing ATSP0's multi-second auto-search, late replies were discarded by the in-flight guard, and the next poll aborted the search with STOPPED, forever; (2) the decoder discarded any chunk containing SEARCHING/STOPPED even when valid data followed in the same chunk — decode('SEARCHING...\r41 0C 1A F8') was nil.
completion_criteria: ELM327Handshake gains a bind step: 0100 after ATSP0; success only on a real '41 00' answer (even inside a SEARCHING chunk); UNABLE/NO DATA/timeout retries then fails AT THE BIND, so the UI says 'the vehicle did not answer (ignition on?)' instead of blaming the adapter
Transport gives the bind a 10s leash and a distinct journal message; READY now means adapter verified AND vehicle answering
OBDPIDDecoder decodes per-line: markers are skipped, never chunk-fatal
Polling: a STOPPED reply re-asks the SAME PID after 300ms (max 2) instead of marching on
Field regressions: the Fozzy journal replayed as fixtures (SEARCHING-chunk bind + polling decode; silent-vehicle fails as bind-context)
testing_criteria: OBDTranscriptReplayTests + ELM327HandshakeTests updated to the bind contract; full suite green; the real proof is the next driveway session

result.notes: Diagnosed from the field journal + code, proven at the pure layer before touching the transport. NOT field-verified yet — W-014 stays in_progress until the Fozzy's gauges move.

items.title: Classifiers anchored, registered, and overridable; the flagged-pull door opens (Fable fix #2)
items.why: Fable's fourth sighting: 'calibrating values while guessing classifiers just relocates the guess.' Proven misroutes: a 2005 Subaru Baja Turbo and a 2019 water-cooled Beetle both classified as air-cooled VW Type 1s — coolant warnings silently suppressed and telemetry refused on liquid-cooled OBD-II cars. And the until-resolved pull flag's only offered door ('Adjust operating envelope') provably didn't clear it; the only real resolution was another WOT pull.
completion_criteria: PlatformBaseline matching is make-anchored with a year window (Type 1 capped at 2003); no entry can match across makes, so a source can never be cited for the wrong marque
isAirCooled: catalog OR the owner's own engine description; safe direction preserved (unknown = liquid-cooled)
supportsOBD2: owner override (Steward Inputs toggle) ?? heuristic — a swapped classic or gray import beats the year rule
Flagged pulls gain the mechanically honest door: 'Acknowledge — inspected & resolved' (first option), dated and logged to the biography; observation clears on acknowledgment OR a later clean pull
STEWARD_THRESHOLDS.md gains a Classifiers & heuristics register section — every routing rule now carries provenance like the values do
testing_criteria: ClassifierAnchorTests: both proven misroutes dead, Tim's Baja and Fozzy still match, air-cooled Porsche honored without VW attribution, override wins both ways, acknowledge is offered first and ACTUALLY clears (the effectiveness contract), clean-pull path intact
full suite green

items.title: Sync bridge — append-only records survive document adoption; TD-001 promoted (Fable fix #3)
items.why: Fable's re-review: 'hardware going live multiplied the concurrent-write exposure the same day it validated the picker.' The phone now writes pull reports during driveway sessions while the Mac holds spec edits — the exact pattern whole-document LWW punishes, with the losing side's work in a snapshot the owner must notice.
completion_criteria: GarageMerge.adopt(remote:preservingAppendsFrom:): adopting a remote document carries over local-only pullReports, performanceRecords, buildEvents, notes, and photos (union by id, adopting side wins collisions)

Deliberate limits stated in code and tests: vehicle set stays adopt-side-wins (no whole-car resurrection); scalars/parts/maintenance stay LWW; same-id edit races stay LWW; add-wins can resurrect a remotely-deleted record (documented trade-off, pinned by test)
applyRemote merges and, when local appends survived, schedules a push so both sides converge
TD-001 PROMOTED to top of the board in TECH_DEBT.md — event sync with tombstones is the real fix; this is the bridge
testing_criteria: GarageMergeTests: driveway-pull-survives (the headline), notes union without dupes, same-id LWW, vehicle-set adopt-wins, resurrection trade-off pinned
full suite green; iOS build green; deployed

items.title: Dyno wheel-hp sanity guard — the other reasoning spine (Fable red-team fix)
items.why: Adversarial honesty pass. OdometerSanity guards the odometer with a non-blocking entry-time caution because one fat-fingered reading poisons every mileage-based claim. Wheel horsepower is the OTHER reasoning spine — it decides hasMeasuredPower, the 'N whp measured' headline, gain-over-stock, cost-per-hp, and the LLM grounding line 'Measured N whp [Strong evidence]' — but had NO such guard; the only gate was > 0. Proven by construction: a 5,000 whp typo flows through as '5000 whp (measured)' and is handed to the LLM as strong-evidence fact. The measuredDynoRecords comment even claimed a 'physically possible' screen the code never enforced.
completion_criteria: DynoAnomaly + Vehicle.dynoAnomaly(proposingWheelHorsepower:) — pure, non-blocking, absolute plausibility ceiling (implausibleWheelHorsepower = 2000), mirroring OdometerAnomaly/implausibleMilesPerDay
A calm caution wired into the dyno wheel-hp entry field (warn, never block — the record is the owner's)
The overreaching measuredDynoRecords comment corrected to say where the screen actually happens (at entry), not to claim a bound the display gate does not enforce
testing_criteria: DynoSanityTests: realistic passes quietly, nil is not an anomaly, boundary (2000 ok / 2001 flags), non-positive, slipped-digit (4770), and a construction test pinning the exact absurd-stated-as-measured-fact leak; full suite green, zero warnings

result.notes: Found one function over from the odometer guard — the honesty discipline had been applied to odometer input but not dyno input. Fix keeps the owner's data (parity with OdometerSanity), warns at entry. Phase 1 of the 'both, in sequence' plan; TD-001 tombstones is phase 2.

items.title: TD-001 phase 1 — tombstones close the sync resurrection hole
items.why: Phase 2 of the 'both, in sequence' plan. The W-054 bridge preserved append-only records across whole-document adoption but left add-wins resurrection as a documented trade-off: a record deleted on one device came back because the other still held it, and GarageMerge's union could not tell a deletion from an append. Live telemetry (phone writes pulls during a session while the Mac holds spec edits) made this the highest-loss hole on the board.
completion_criteria: Vehicle.deletedRecordIDs — a synced Set<UUID> of tombstones for the five append collections (pull reports, performance records, build events, notes, photos); Codable decodes a missing set to empty so old documents load
Deletion routes through mutating helpers that remove AND tombstone: deletePerformanceRecord, deleteNotes, deletePhoto, and removeServiceRecord; the three view delete sites call them

GarageMerge unions both sides tombstones and suppresses any id they name from either side — a deletion on either device propagates instead of being undone by the other held copy; delete-wins is deliberate (UUIDs unique per creation)
Residual limit stated in code and test: an absence WITHOUT a tombstone is still preserved as an append (a pre-tombstone client cannot have deletions honored) — only full event history closes that
testing_criteria: GarageMergeTests: rewrote the old resurrection trade-off test to assert delete-wins; added local-delete propagation, tombstone union, delete-wins-over-held-copy, untombstoned-absence limit, helper-records-tombstone, and Codable round-trip + legacy-default; full suite green, zero warnings, iOS app builds

result.notes: Phase 1 of TD-001: the deletion half. Scalar/parts/maintenance edit races remain LWW (documented) and need real per-record timestamps — the next TD-001 step. Not field-tested against two live devices yet; logic pinned by tests.

items.title: Deep-review hardening — CloudKit truth, OBD diagnosis, deterministic fallback, schema and CI
items.why: Codex's 2026-07-19 independent review found two whole-garage sync hazards (failed pull treated as not-found; failed conflict retry reported successful), a field report that falsely diagnosed an answering ELM processor as silent, future-schema downgrade exposure, a dropped StewardContext in the keyword fallback, and CI that did not enforce the documented release/zero-warning bar.
completion_criteria: Cloud pull distinguishes found, not-found, unreadable, and failed; only explicit not-found may seed cloud
Cloud conflict retry reports success only after a confirmed save
OBD journal distinguishes ELM/configuration success from vehicle-bind failure and records sanitized bind outcomes/protocol diagnostics without raw telemetry or identifiers
Future schema documents are preserved as unsupported rather than decoded and later downgraded
Steward keyword fallback uses the caller's injected StewardContext
CI enforces warnings and compiles the Release simulator configuration; TD-004 reflects real Veepeak evidence
testing_criteria: Regression tests cover typed cloud-pull decisions, failed conflict-save truth, the supplied Veepeak bind-failure diagnosis, future-schema refusal, and injected fallback time
full suite green, zero source warnings, Debug and Release iOS Simulator builds green

result.tests: 458 -> 464, +6; warnings-as-errors; Debug + Release iOS Simulator builds green
result.notes: The original Veepeak bind-failure report is pinned as a field regression. Two subsequent July 19 reports confirmed a supported 41 00 response, polling, and measured data in 2.4 and 2.7 seconds. CloudKit network and server behavior still require real-container integration validation; pure failure decisions and persistence boundaries are regression-tested.

items.title: Version recovery snapshots with the portable garage schema
items.why: Conflict and pre-restore safety copies still encoded the legacy bare vehicle array after the live garage and CloudKit payload moved to a versioned document. A recovery artifact should preserve the same explicit schema boundary as the truth it protects.
completion_criteria: New conflict and pre-restore snapshots encode through GaragePersistence
Recovery discovery and restore continue accepting legacy bare-array snapshots
A newly created pre-restore safety copy decodes as the current versioned document
testing_criteria: RecoverySnapshotTests prove legacy restore, versioned safety-copy encoding, discovery counts, unreadable refusal, undoable restore, and deletion; full suite green with warnings as errors

result.notes: This changes only recovery-file serialization. The primary garage format and backward-compatible decoder are unchanged. TD-004 was also corrected to record the two successful measured-data Veepeak sessions.

items.title: Collapse duplicate OBD connection-journal transitions
items.why: The July 19 successful Veepeak report logged the same PROTOCOL transition four times at +2.1s. Those were owner-facing render refreshes during sequential ELM configuration commands, not four retries, so the support report made a healthy handshake look stalled.
completion_criteria: Consecutive entries with the same stage and message are stored once
A later identical transition after a real intervening state remains visible, preserving reconnect evidence
The successful field timeline reads as distinct milestones rather than repeated render refreshes
testing_criteria: OBDConnectionJournalTests pins both duplicate collapse and later retry preservation; full suite green with warnings as errors; Debug and Release iOS Simulator builds green

result.tests: 464 -> 465, +1; warnings-as-errors; Debug + Release iOS Simulator builds green
result.notes: Fix derived directly from the 503.6-second measured-data Veepeak field report. Only consecutive exact duplicates are collapsed; different messages and repeated states after an intervening transition remain durable.
